sutta: mounts lay disciples should they wish may express disapproval of the monk who is possessed of 8 qualities 1 aid he goes about to bring loss to householders he goes about to bring harm to householders he reviles and defames them he causes this union or disharmony between householder and householder he speaks in this praise of the buddha of dhamma of the sangha they see him in evil horns monks lay disciples should they wish may express disapproval of a monk who is possessed of these 8 qualities now this is the reverse of the earlier sutta this 1 is that if a manga has certain bad qualities similar bad qualities as mentioned earlier that means it brings loss to householders it brings harm to householders he reviles and defames them he causes this harmony between householder and householder speaks in displays of buddha dhamma and sangha or he goes to he frequents uh evil haunts this last 1 he frequents evil horns means he often goes to certain places which are not proper for a monk for example always going to visit a prostitute who is going to visit widow always going to visit an unmarried lady always going to visit a homosexual or you know and another 1 always go these uh if he does these things then lay person can express disapproval express disapproval means they bring it up to some senior manga to complain about this monk or they speak to the monk directly and
